physicalDetails.detail: An older road trader in a patched coat and broad hat with a broken nose set crooked for good, driving an open wagon silvered with grain dust.

voiceMarkers.detail: Plainspoken and practical, with the cadence of a man who knows settlements by market habits, bridge conditions, and flour prices rather than by rhetoric.

beliefs.belief: Millhaven should have been ordinary enough to trade with six weeks ago and shocking enough to stop him cold now.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Keep his wagon moving north after the brief exchange in Millhaven.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Momentarily startled by the empty village, then practical again once the road talk is done.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Stops when he sees armed Farstriders standing in an empty village and answers Sylvanas's questions about the road ahead.
Continues north after giving the party local trade information and road conditions.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Carries credible route knowledge about settlements, bridges, flour shortages, and recent market conditions along the road between Millhaven and Corin's Crossing.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: His surprise breaks through a forgettable road face only briefly before habit and wagon work settle back over it.

knowledge.detail: Knows Millhaven traded normally six weeks earlier and that Corin's Crossing was still open, coughing, and functioning four days before he passed north.
Recognizes Farstriders on sight and understands enough of the road network to know plague here means settlements south are in danger without yet knowing it.

By chapter5 Sylvanas recognizes that he came north out of the same feeder-road web now leading them toward the Barov-linked depots, which makes his earlier road knowledge feel more charged than incidental.

Chapter6 retroactively sharpens him again: another grain man on the Andorhal approach uses the exact same farewell, turning the old seller's parting words into a recurring road signal rather than a memorable one-off.

arc.currentPosition: Still unresolved on-page, but chapter6 upgrades him again from suspicious memory to part of a repeated road pattern. He remains a clue, not a solved identity.
